No Poverty.reason: Calculus provides foundational quantitative tools for economics and public finance, which are essential for developing pro-poor policies and social protection systems (Target 1.3, 1.b).

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links Calculus (I) to ending hunger or improving food systems.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course's application in 'statistics' provides foundational tools for epidemiology and public health analysis, supporting capacity for managing health risks (Target 3.d).

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops '分析與解決問題的能力' (analytical skills) crucial for employment (Target 4.4).
evidence: 分析與解決問題的能力

Gender Equality.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links Calculus (I) to gender equality initiatives or outcomes.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links Calculus (I) to water or sanitation issues.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: Calculus applied in 'economics' provides tools to model energy markets and policies, relevant to improving energy efficiency and renewable energy adoption (Target 7.2, 7.3).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course is foundational for 'economics' and 'public finance,' directly supporting analysis of economic growth, productivity, and financial knowledge (Targets 8.1, 8.2, Core Competence A).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Calculus is a fundamental tool for scientific research and innovation (Target 9.5), and the course develops '分析與解決問題的能力' (analytical skills) essential for technological advancement.
evidence: 分析與解決問題的能力

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Applications in 'public finance' are key to designing fiscal and social protection policies aimed at achieving greater equality (Target 10.4).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The application in 'public finance' provides tools for analyzing funding and resource allocation for sustainable urban infrastructure and services (Target 11.2).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course teaches 'Lagrange Multiplier' optimization, applicable in economics to model efficient resource use and sustainable management (Target 12.2).

Climate Action.reason: Foundational tools for 'economics' and 'public finance' are used to model and implement climate change measures, such as carbon taxes and policy analysis (Target 13.2).

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links Calculus (I) to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links Calculus (I) to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: As a course for the 'Public Finance' department, it provides analytical tools for building effective, accountable, and transparent public institutions (Target 16.6, Core Competence D).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Supports 'public finance' for domestic resource mobilization (Target 17.1) and 'statistics' for data capacity-building (Target 17.19), both crucial for SDG implementation.
